title: TLS 1.3, Certificate Identity, ALPN, and HTTPS · Mastery Check
summary: A security review compares near-miss parser decisions for tls 1.3, certificate identity, alpn, and https and requires the smallest strict TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record with changed-input evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for TLS identity, negotiation, and protocol boundary record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish TCP connection, TLS records, handshake, authenticated peer identity, traffic keys, alerts, application data, and closure.
Validate certificate chain, time, DNS or IP reference identity, key usage, revocation policy, and trust roots without insecure skip flags.
Offer and select application protocols with ALPN before application data and reject unsupported or inconsistent negotiation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Tls Record Handshake Boundary · changed case · Quiz Tls Record Handshake Boundary
